items.title: Congressmen call for National Guard to address drone threat at World Cup
items.summary: Congressmen call for immediate interagency action to secure airspace over ‌the 11 US ​World Cup 2026 host cities.

items.title: Kashmir seminary declared unlawful under anti-terror law, sparks outcry
items.summary: Kashmiri political and religious leaders denounced the move as part of a broader pattern of New Delhi’s overreach.

items.title: Iraq appoints new prime minister-designate
items.summary: Iraq appoints new prime minister-designate

items.title: Croatia’s Modric undergoes facial surgery but aims to be fit for World Cup
items.summary: Luka Modric suffered a facial ⁠fracture in AC Milan's game against Juventus and underwent surgery the following day.

items.title: All New York boroughs to host free World Cup fan events, Mamdani says
items.summary: Amid exorbitant fares and match tickets, fan events in New York will be free during the World Cup.

items.title: How Iran has changed, and how it hasn’t, in two months of war
items.summary: Common Iranians face job losses and shortages. But key institutions remain in place, with hardliners stronger now.

items.title: Before World Cup, FIFA slammed for politicising sport with Trump prize
items.summary: Football officials and players question FIFA’s decision to award its inaugural Peace Prize to US President Donald Trump.

items.title: FIFA urged to push US for ‘ICE Truce’ at World Cup by Human Rights Watch
items.summary: ICE has represented the US immigration crackdown and deportation drive, but HRW wants a truce for duration of World Cup.

items.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Under cover of ceasefire, Israel increases grip
items.summary: Settler violence surges deeper into Palestinian-administered areas, and Gaza's police forces are decimated further.

items.title: Down but not out: In war with Israel, Hezbollah shows it is still powerful
items.summary: Despite setbacks, Hezbollah emerges as a key player, tied to Lebanon’s stability and US-Iran negotiations.

items.title: ‘Should be fired’: Why the Trumps want Jimmy Kimmel sacked
items.summary: Trump calls for Kimmel's firing over remarks tied to Melania and a recent shooting incident at a media event.

items.title: Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

items.title: Athletics great Allyson Felix aiming to compete at 2028 Los Angeles Games
items.summary: Felix, the most decorated woman in Olympic athletics history with 11 medals, plans a comeback at age 40 at the Olympics.

items.title: UK to appeal High Court ruling that granted Palestine Action a victory
items.summary: The Home Office is challenging judges who ruled the proscription of the group as a terrorist organisation as unlawful

items.title: Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over ‘stolen’ grain shipments
items.summary: The diplomatic spat unfolds as Ukrainian drones hit Russia's Tuapse oil refinery, causing a 'massive' fire.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 60 as diplomacy gathers pace?
items.summary: Trump team reviews Iran peace plan to reopen Hormuz with nuclear talks potentially delayed to a later stage.

items.title: Trump administration fires all members of US’ National Science Board
items.summary: Democrats blast latest move by the administration to radically restructure the federal government.

items.title: At least 20 killed in Colombia highway blast
items.summary: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has blamed a 'narco-terrorist group', led by a former FARC fighter, for the attack.

items.title: New Zealand spy plane reports possible North Korea sanctions breach at sea
items.summary: New Zealand's Defence Force says a North Korean ship was observed engaging in a possible transfer of illicit goods at sea.

items.title: Tailgating to be allowed at Boston World Cup matches as FIFA changes stance
items.summary: Boston World Cup host committee says fans will be allowed to tailgate for all seven matches at the Gillette Stadium.

items.title: Oil prices rise despite Iran’s proposal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude rises 3 percent despite Iran's offer to reopen waterway in exchange for deferral of nuclear talks.

items.title: Gunmen kill at least 29 in Nigeria’s northeast Adamawa State
items.summary: The ISIL affiliate in the region has claimed responsibility for the attack on Guyaku village, which lasted for hours.

items.title: Mexican military captures cartel commander Audias Flores
items.summary: The Mexican military released footage of an operation resulting in the capture of Audias Flores.

items.title: Mexico’s Jalisco drug cartel commander ‘El Jardinero’ found hiding in ditch
items.summary: Mexican Navy says special forces apprehended Jalisco New Generation Cartel leader 'without a single shot being fired'.
